summary: DHH celebrates Instiki, his wiki software, reaching 5,000 downloads. He highlights an endorsement from Mena Trott of Six Apart, who uses Instiki for personal note-taking rather than collaboration. DHH acknowledges he has been neglecting Instiki in favor of other projects like TextMate, Ruby on Rails, and Basecamp, but believes the product has much more growth potential.
key_points: Instiki reached 5,000 downloads, a milestone for the early open source wiki tool
Mena Trott of Six Apart endorsed Instiki as a personal note-taking tool, finding it better suited for individual use than collaborative wikis
DHH was already splitting his attention across multiple projects including Rails and Basecamp, foreshadowing his future priorities
The product grew organically despite its creator's neglect, suggesting strong product-market fit
money_quotes: I'm thrilled to see how Instiki just keeps on growing even as I'm shamelessly neglecting it for other pastimes.
Instiki has at least 10x the user base in it.
key_insight: A good product can grow organically even when its creator is distracted by other projects, which in this case happened to be Ruby on Rails and Basecamp.
